Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 106 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. Teaching Claude to QA a mobile app 72 upvotes, 7 comments
  3. How to Attract AI Bots to Your Open Source Project 92 upvotes, 13 comments
  4. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 169 upvotes, 35 comments
  5. Intuitions for Tranformer Circuits 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. GrapheneOS will remain usable by anyone without requiring personal information 268 upvotes, 72 comments
  7. Ordered Dithering with Arbitrary or Irregular Colour Palettes (2023) 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. 25 Years of Eggs 256 upvotes, 73 comments
  9. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 381 upvotes, 115 comments
  10. I Reverse-Engineered the TiinyAI Pocket Lab from Marketing Photos 47 upvotes, 10 comments
  11. Migrating the American Express Payment Network, Twice 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  12. "Collaboration" Is Bullshit 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  13. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 317 upvotes, 108 comments
  14. Five Years of Running a Systems Reading Group at Microsoft 128 upvotes, 38 comments
  15. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 246 upvotes, 82 comments
  16. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 146 upvotes, 53 comments
  17. PC Gamer recommends RSS readers in a 37mb article that just keeps downloading 395 upvotes, 187 comments
  18. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 176 upvotes, 82 comments
  19. LLMs predict my coffee 86 upvotes, 37 comments
  20. The future of version control 442 upvotes, 253 comments
  21. Answer Engine Optimization 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. Why I love NixOS 231 upvotes, 154 comments
  23. First and Lego Education Partnership Update 29 upvotes, 13 comments
  24. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 294 upvotes, 228 comments
  25. Show HN: Codala, a social network built on scanning barcodes 35 upvotes, 19 comments
  26. They're Vibe-Coding Spam Now 49 upvotes, 33 comments
  27. Windows native app development is a mess 355 upvotes, 359 comments
  28. The hottest new phone is Tin Can, a 'landline' for kids 28 upvotes, 24 comments
  29. What Young Workers Are Doing to AI-Proof Themselves 88 upvotes, 114 comments
  30. You are not your job 82 upvotes, 115 comments

Last generated 11:30am, 23 March 2026. Source here.
